sourceEvidence.label: Intertrochanteric Fracture — Learning objectives
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Learning objectives for Intertrochanteric Fracture

objectives: Recognize stable versus unstable intertrochanteric fracture patterns.
Select sliding hip screw versus cephalomedullary nail fixation concepts.
Identify implant failure and varus collapse risks.

payload.oneLiner: Intertrochanteric fractures are extracapsular injuries where stability pattern drives implant selection.
pimpQuestions: What makes an intertrochanteric fracture unstable?
When is a cephalomedullary nail preferred?
orSurvivalTips: Use tip-apex distance language when discussing lag screw

extractedSignals.summary: 10 high-confidence Anki card mapping(s) for curriculum node trauma-intertrochanteric-fractures
40 Orthobullets question mapping(s) for trauma-intertrochanteric-fractures (metadata only — no stems/answers)

metadata.claim_text: Reverse obliquity intertrochanteric fractures are unstable patterns where sliding hip screw fixation commonly fails.
Varus collapse and lag-screw cutout are classic failure modes after intertrochanteric fixation.

metadata.description: Intramedullary implant for unstable intertrochanteric and subtrochanteric fracture patterns.
Plate-and-screw construct for stable intertrochanteric fracture patterns.
Operative intramedullary fixation procedure using a cephalomedullary nail.
Dense medial femoral neck cortical buttress supporting head-neck load transfer.
Extracapsular metaphyseal zone between neck and subtrochanteric diaphysis.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Medial Femoral Circumflex Artery

metadata.description: Dominant retinacular blood supply to the femoral head; vulnerable in neck fractures.

metadata.claim_text: Calcar and medial cortical buttress integrity strongly influence intertrochanteric fracture stability.
Intertrochanteric fractures are extracapsular injuries where stability pattern drives sliding hip screw versus cephalomedullary nail selection.
Treating an unstable intertrochanteric pattern like a stable extra-articular fracture without implant selection adjustment.

metadata.action: Favor cephalomedullary nail fixation with attending confirmation of implant and reduction strategy
metadata.trigger: Unstable intertrochanteric pattern, reverse obliquity, or poor medial cortical support

metadata.claim_text: Describe fracture line orientation, medial comminution, implant category, tip-apex distance language, and mobilization plan.

metadata.action: Proceed with sliding hip screw pathway when attending agrees stability assessment
metadata.trigger: Stable intertrochanteric pattern with intact medial buttress suitable for sliding hip screw

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Proximal Femur Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite proximal femur model linking head, neck, trochanters, calcar, and hip joint.
Medial proximal femur attachment for iliopsoas; key subtrochanteric landmark.
Posterior hip nerve at risk during proximal femur exposure and reduction maneuvers.
Radiographic pattern with posterior/medial comminution or reverse obliquity suggesting instability.
Cutout, varus collapse, or fixation failure after intertrochanteric fracture treatment.
Extracapsular proximal femur fracture through the intertrochanteric region.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Reverse Obliquity Intertrochanteric Fracture

metadata.description: Reverse oblique intertrochanteric line with medial cortical failure risk.
Lateral proximal femur with abductor insertions; intertrochanteric fixation landmark.
Ball-and-socket articulation between femoral head and acetabulum.
Articular femoral head supplied largely by retinacular and circumflex vessels.
Intracapsular segment between femoral head and intertrochanteric region.
